source.source_note: GIMPS lists 52 known Mersenne primes; the latest listed frontier exponent is 136,279,841.

historical_local_calibration.method: For each known high Mersenne exponent, compare its LJPW score against nearby prime exponents without using Mersenne proof data.

filters.small_factor_filter: For prime exponent p, scan q = 2kp + 1 through k limit; only q == 1 or 7 mod 8 can divide M_p, then test pow(2, p, q) == 1.
filters.small_factor_limit_note: Passing this screen is not evidence of primality; it only means no factor was found in this bounded small-factor pass.

transition_branch_scout.method: Fit high-exponent known Mersenne exponent ratios to the LJPW grammar, apply the strongest transition templates to the current frontier exponent, snap each projection to nearby prime exponents, then run the same bounded small-factor screen.

extended_small_factor_validation.method: Re-screen the top immediate candidates and top transition branches through a deeper k limit.

interpretation.plain_read: The scout does not find or prove the next Mersenne prime. It creates a relationally ordered short list of prime exponents beyond the current record that also survive a bounded small-factor screen.
interpretation.next_required_step: Run real PRP or Lucas-Lehmer style Mersenne tests on the top surviving exponents, preferably through GIMPS-compatible tooling so results are reproducible and comparable.
